question: Tom had some money. He spent $15 on food, then gave half of the remainder to his sister. He then spent $20 on a book and had $35 left. How much money did Tom have at first?

There are some boys and girls in a hall. There are 3 times as many boys as girls. Each boy receives 3 balloons while each girl receives 5 balloons. A total of 70 balloons were given out. How many boys are there?
A teacher has some pencils. If she gives 5 pencils to each student, she will be short of 8 pencils. If she gives 4 pencils to each student, she will have 12 pencils left. How many students are there?
There are chickens and rabbits in a farm. There are 35 heads and 94 legs altogether. How many rabbits are there?
Sarah bought some apples and oranges. Apples cost $2 each and oranges cost $3 each. She bought 15 fruits altogether and paid $38. How many apples did she buy?
A number is multiplied by 4, then 12 is added. The result is divided by 2, then 5 is subtracted. The final answer is 21. What was the original number?
In a class, if each student gets 6 stickers, there will be 10 stickers left. If each student gets 7 stickers, there will be 5 stickers short. How many students are in the class?
There are 20 questions in a test. Each correct answer scores 5 points and each wrong answer deducts 2 points. John scored 72 points. How many questions did he answer correctly?

A box contains red and blue marbles. There are 4 times as many red marbles as blue marbles. If 10 red marbles are removed and 5 blue marbles are added, there will be equal number of red and blue marbles. How many red marbles were there originally?

Mrs. Tan baked some cookies. She gave half of them to her neighbours, then ate 3 cookies. She then gave half of the remainder to her children and had 8 cookies left. How many cookies did she bake at first?
There are some $2 and $5 notes in a wallet. There are 25 notes altogether worth $98. How many $5 notes are there?
A group of children shared some candies. If each child gets 8 candies, there will be 6 candies left. If each child gets 9 candies, there will be 4 candies short. How many candies were there?
There are 3 times as many boys as girls in a club. Each boy pays $4 membership fee and each girl pays $6. The total collected was $216. How many members are there altogether?
A farmer has cows and ducks. There are 50 animals altogether. There are 140 legs altogether. How many cows are there?
Tom saved some money in January. In February, he saved twice as much as January. In March, he saved $30 less than February. He saved $270 altogether in 3 months. How much did he save in January?
There are some benches in a park. If 4 people sit on each bench, 6 people will be standing. If 5 people sit on each bench, 1 bench will be empty. How many people are there?
A shop sells pens at $3 each and pencils at $1 each. John bought 20 items altogether. He paid $44. How many more pens than pencils did he buy?

There are some red, blue and green balls in a bag. There are twice as many red balls as blue balls. There are 5 more green balls than blue balls. Altogether there are 65 balls. How many red balls are there?
